metadata.title: Learning to Reach Goals via Diffusion

content: Generate trajectories that "diffuse away" from potential goals, and train a policy to reverse them

figures.description: Denoising Diffusion process showing noise sample transitioning to a target dog image
Diagram of goal-conditioned RL showing forward and reverse diffusion paths between initial and goal states

sections.title: Goal Conditioned RL as Diffusion
content: Diffusion models:map any point from Gaussian → data manifold
Goal-conditioned RL:learn optimal path from any state → goal state
Actions that lead us away from the goal in place of Gaussian noise; Policy that reverses these actions in place of score function

figures.description: Mathematical state transition diagram comparing diffusion model and GCRL processes

sections.title: A Simple 2D Navigation Problem
content: Task:Navigate to goal X;States:(x,y);Actions:unit length displacement
Forward process:Take random actions starting from goal
Reverse process:Train policy to reverse the forward trajectories
(a) Visualization of trajectories, (b) Merlin policy actions, (c) GCSL policy actions
Backward view of diffusion gives us control over the goal distribution

figures.description: Visualization of forward diffusion trajectories starting from goal X
Sampled actions from Merlin diffusion policy
Mean actions from GCSL policy for comparison

sections.title: Effect of Time Conditioning
content: Analogous to the score function in diffusion, the Merlin policy is conditioned on time
This time indicates how "noisy" current state is, or how far we are from target
Policy takes optimal action far from goal and becomes uncertain as it approaches
The time conditioning affects policy uncertainty based on expected number of steps to reach the goal

sections.title: Optimization Objective
content: Given an offline dataset D, we view trajectories as forward diffusion chains.
Behavior cloning maximizes the probability of reaching goal states:
θ* = arg max E [log πθ(a | s, g)]

sections.title: Constructing the Forward Process
content: Merlin:Use pre-collected offline trajectories, train policy using BC
Merlin-P:Learn reverse dynamics model to simulate trajectories backward
Merlin-NP:Use proposed trajectory stitching method

figures.description: Visualization of trajectory stitching for constructing backward trajectories

sections.title: Experimental Results
content: Merlin outperforms previous methods, while being highly-efficient!

figures.description: Bar chart showing Merlin outperforms others in normalized returns
Bar chart showing Merlin inference time efficiency

sections.title: Conclusions and future work
content: Merlin constructs trajectories that "diffuse away" from goals and trains a policy to reverse them
Simple training dynamics indicate potential for scalability as shown by denoising diffusion models
Future work includes extending to online setting and partially observable environments
